heading: Training and services to end abuse in later life
label: 12421 Training and services to end abuse in later life
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 14041 of Title 42

children.label: i
children.text: provide training programs to assist law enforcement agencies, prosecutors, agencies of States or units of local government, population specific organizations, victim service providers, victim advocates, or relevant officers in Federal, tribal, State, territorial, and local courts in recognizing and addressing instances of abuse in later life;

children.label: ii
children.text: provide or enhance services for victims of abuse in later life;

children.label: iii
children.text: establish or support multidisciplinary collaborative community responses to victims of abuse in later life; and

children.label: iv
children.text: conduct cross-training for law enforcement agencies, prosecutors, agencies of States or units of local government, attorneys, health care providers, population specific organizations, faith-based leaders, victim advocates, victim service providers, courts, and first responders to better serve older victims.

children.label: i
children.text: provide training programs to assist attorneys, health care providers, faith-based leaders, community-based organizations, or other professionals who may identify or respond to abuse in later life; or

children.label: ii
children.text: conduct outreach activities and awareness campaigns to ensure that victims of abuse in later life receive appropriate assistance.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: C Waiver
children.text: The Attorney General may waive 1 or more of the activities described in subparagraph (A) upon making a determination that the activity would duplicate services available in the community.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: D Limitation
children.text: An eligible entity receiving a grant under this section may use not more than 10 percent of the total funds received under the grant for an activity described in subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.label: i
children.text: a State;

children.label: ii
children.text: a unit of local government;

children.label: iii
children.text: a tribal government or tribal organization;

children.label: iv
children.text: a population specific organization;

children.label: v
children.text: a victim service provider; or

children.label: vi
children.text: a State, tribal, or territorial domestic violence or sexual assault coalition; and

children.label: i
children.text: a law enforcement agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: a prosecutor’s office;

children.label: iii
children.text: a victim service provider; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a nonprofit program or government agency with demonstrated experience in assisting individuals 50 years of age or over.

children.heading: Underserved populations
children.label: 3 Underserved populations
children.text: In making grants under this section, the Attorney General shall give priority to proposals providing services to culturally specific and underserved populations.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
